repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md says live PartialID catalogue is initially empty

published_prior_art.cite: DingLu2017 and JiangYangDing2022: principal-score point identification under principal ignorability; this is the Gamma=1 calibration corner

repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest banked ordinal-IV proposal has a different response-path object

published_prior_art.cite: NguyenStuartScharfsteinOgburn2024: binary one-sided PI sensitivity; DornGuoKallus2022: odds-ratio sharp-bound technology for unmeasured confounding

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1 Conjecture 1 is closest, but concerns ordinal-IV response paths rather than posterior principal-stratum weights calibrated to pi

published_prior_art.cite: WuAntonelli2024 and TongKahanHarhayLi2025 are closest, but neither gives a finite K>=3 ordinal adjacency posterior-chain compatibility LP

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add an explicit K=3 finite-support witness showing strict containment of the edgewise binary relaxation, with nonconstant Y and positive mass in every relevant stratum.
Add one paragraph comparing Schwartz-Li-Reiter 2012 and rank-based principal-stratum sensitivity work to preempt older binary sensitivity prior art.
State a follow-on variant where the known principal-score vector pi is replaced by a supplied principal-score polytope, so the field-tier claim is less dependent on externally known pi.

recommended_next_step: Proceed with Conjecture 1 as the flagship; in Stage 0/1 prioritize the K=3 witness and the Bayes-disintegration sharpness construction.
